evidence_assessments.supported_claim: Cover page and table of contents; no information about fault output or output logic.
Index continuation; no technical content about fault output or failure logic.
States the unit offers three O.C. alarm outputs 'and a fourth O.C. output for Fault', confirming the fault output exists.
Mounting and circuit layout; the layout listed does not describe the fault output or its logic.
Cable sizing and control panel connection guidance; does not address fault output behavior.
Italian description confirms 'una uscita Open Collector associata al guasto' (an O.C. output associated with fault).
Confirms a Fault Output (o.c. 40 mA) and explains outputs are Normally Energised (fail-safe), so they de-energize on fault; answers both parts of the question.

Shows terminal 8 'Uscita O.C. Guasto / O.C. Fault output' and states outputs are 'in sicurezza positiva, con commutazione a negativo' (positive safety, negative switching), covering both fault output and logic.
Describes negative-safety (JN) TWIN variant behavior at power on, giving context that output logic varies by version.
Italian wiring/connection guidance; does not address fault output or failure logic.

set_judgements.rationale: The set fully answers: E-5D790265BC9C confirms a dedicated O.C. fault output (40 mA) and states outputs are Normally Energised (fail-safe), so on failure they release. E-711819F57BE5 corroborates with terminal 8 fault output and 'sicurezza positiva' logic. Description pages confirm the fourth O.C. fault output. A single page-8 item is sufficient on its own.

evidence_assessments.supported_claim: Cable sizing and connection advice; no fault output or failure logic content.
Packing instructions; unrelated to the question.
Mounting orientation and circuit layout; does not describe fault output logic.
Confirms the unit offers 'a fourth O.C. output for Fault'.
Product code table shows open-collector mode options: P=positive safety (energized), N=negative safety (not energized), clarifying output logic variants.
Describes negative-safety TWIN variant LED/output behavior, contextual to output logic versions.
Cover page and index; no relevant technical facts.
States detectors have Normally Energised O.C. outputs associated with alarm thresholds and fault status, and lists 'Fault Output: o.c. 40 mA'; fully answers both parts.
Testing and use procedures; no fault output logic content.
Technical specs confirm self-diagnostic routine with 40 mA O.C. fault output and output logic 'Sicurezza Positiva / Fail safe (standard) or fail in alarm'.

set_judgements.rationale: The set completely answers the question: E-94461ECDE221 confirms the dedicated 40 mA O.C. fault output and that outputs are Normally Energised (fail-safe), so they de-energize on failure. E-F799B3DC568B independently confirms the fault output and fail-safe (or fail-in-alarm) output logic. Either item alone suffices; E-94461ECDE221 is selected as the smallest sufficient set.
